dealKiller: Load-bearing assumption falsified: Italy's ReGiS (Sogei/MEF-RGS) and Greece's OPS-TA (Greece 2.0) are free, mandatory, state-built integrated information systems that ARE the recipient-side compliance portal. They handle monitoring, milestone/target tracking, DNSH evidence upload, expense rendicontazione, and Commission-bound transmission for every beneficiary. The spec's claim that 'Commission/MS will not provide recipient-side compliance tools (creates state liability)' is empirically wrong — both MS shipped these by 2023 and updated them through Circular MEF-RGS 35/Oct-2024 and Circular 33/Jul-2024. StateAidOps is reduced to a thin Excel-replacement wrapper over a portal that already auto-validates monthly. Compounding kill: programme terminates 31-Aug-2026; Italy's 6th revision (Oct-2025) already cuts measures that won't hit deadline; MFF 2028-2034 proposal (16-Jul-2025) replaces RRF/cohesion/CAP with single per-MS National Regional Partnership Plans — destroys the multi-country Annex VI template moat before contract renewal cycle. Big-4 cannibalization is also active (EY 150 agents, Deloitte Zora, PwC GL.ai, KPMG $2B) and bundled into existing audit relationships at TERNA/Metlen/Webuild/Enel where Big-4 already hold the corporate-finance/audit seat (EY-Parthenon on Kavala port is the tell).
patternMatches: sovereign-build-free (the very pattern section 0 of the doc warns about — ReGiS + OPS-TA are the Arachne-equivalents on the recipient side, exactly the gap the spec claimed was open)
programme-end cliff (RRF terminates Aug-2026; 18-month extension request rejected as of Jun-2025; no contractual bridge to MFF 2028-2034)
successor-mandate-discontinuity (NRPPs are single-MS plans, not 27-MS harmonized templates; re-platform once and Big-4 dominate)
incumbent-bundling (Big-4 already advisors of record on named primes; AI-augmentation shipped 2024-2026 across all four)
compliance-as-feature-of-ERP (SAP/Oracle financial close + ReGiS API ingestion makes a third-party SaaS redundant)
category-questionable-buyer (recipient PMOs are not procurement-law-bound to use external — unlike DG-Studies, no Art. 160 lock)
unitEconomicsConcern: €60-150K/yr ACV against a buyer who already pays for ReGiS/OPS-TA via taxes and whose Big-4 auditor will absorb the work into the existing €300K-1.5M engagement at zero marginal price to defend audit fees. Net new spend justification collapses. With programme ending in 15 months from pilot start, payback window is sub-12 months and renewal risk is binary (NRPP transition).
loadBearingAssumption: 'Commission/MS will not provide recipient-side compliance tools' — FALSIFIED. ReGiS (Italy, mandatory per Reg EU 2021/241 Art 22.2.d) and OPS-TA (Greece) both exist, are free, are mandatory, and Sogei is actively expanding ReGiS for the 2021-2027 cohesion period — meaning the same portal extends into the successor MFF.
alternativeThesis: If T-Life still wants exposure to this buyer: invert to a *services* play, not SaaS — a Greek-language boutique that staffs ex-Big-4 RRF-advisory seniors at 1/3 day-rate, sits on top of OPS-TA/ReGiS rather than competing with it, and migrates the practice to MFF 2028-2034 NRPP preparation work in 2027 (where MS need help designing single-plan submissions). This is closer to a discount DG-Studies cousin than a product. Alternatively, reroute the AI layer to *granting-authority side* assurance — selling Arachne-complementary anomaly detection to Greek/Italian audit authorities (EDEL, IGRUE) — which keeps the corpus moat but flips the buyer to a procurement-law-mandated arm's-length entity. Either way: StateAidOps as specified (recipient-side SaaS to named primes) does not survive.
